channel.title: Frontiers in Energy Research | New and Recent Articles
channel.description: Recent articles from Frontiers in Energy Research

item.title: Long-cycle stability of solid-state lithium metal batteries enabled by a fluorinated polymer electrolyte interlayer
item.description: We report a fluorinated poly(ether) electrolyte interlayer that suppresses dendrite nucleation in solid-state lithium metal batteries operated at 0.5 mA/cm^2. Cells assembled with a Li/LiFePO4 chemistry retain 87% of initial capacity after 1,200 cycles at room temperature, more than doubling the lifetime of unmodified Li6PS5Cl baselines. Cryo-EM analysis reveals a 12-nm-thick LiF-rich interphase formed in-situ that mechanically anchors the lithium metal anode. The interlayer is solution-processable at scale and adds less than 4% to areal cell mass. Cost projections at 10 GWh annual production are within 15% of state-of-the-art liquid electrolyte packs.
item.author: Ravi Subramanian

item.title: Editorial: Grid-scale storage pathways for the 2030 transition
